response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer, but his profile does not match this role's mandatory requirements. His professional C++ experience is limited to roughly seven months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 (plus a thesis project), far short of the required 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development. His career centers on web and full-stack work in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. He has no SQL Server or Oracle experience listed, and no fixed income, derivatives, or capital markets domain expertise — his closest exposure is a one-year Node.js role at a German investment fintech, which does not substitute for the required domain depth.
strengths: Broad, sustained senior-level engineering career with strong craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development)
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience at Dunnhumby handling millions of requests per second
Some financial-sector exposure via RIDE Capital investment platform work
concerns: Professional C++ experience is minimal (~7 months) and dated to 2014–2015, versus the mandatory 5–10 years on Windows
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; database background is MySQL and MongoDB
No Fixed Income, derivatives, or capital markets domain knowledge, and no Summit platform exposure

key_factors.factor: Missing fixed income / capital markets domain expertise
Strong general engineering fundamentals and performance systems background
